preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: Startups needing an AI-ready design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes context for startups seeking an AI-ready design system. As a decision-stage page, the Hero uses the with-price variant to surface the offer (price + CTA) above the fold, giving Validators an immediate anchor before they evaluate proof.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators need trust signals early. Placing Credibility second satisfies both the decision-stage prescribed order and the Validator archetype's demand for authority — customer logos, partner badges, and endorsements from recognized startups reduce perceived risk before diving into capabilities.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Outlines the AI-ready design system's core capabilities and outcome promises. For a Validator at the decision stage, this beat reframes features as validated, tangible outcomes — component intelligence, design-to-code accuracy, and integration speed — reinforcing that the product delivers on its above-the-fold promise.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Critical for the Validator archetype. Presents hard metrics, case study data, and proof points (e.g., adoption stats, time-to-ship reductions, startup success stories) that substantiate the Value claims. Required by the decision-stage rules and directly addresses the Validator's need for empirical confirmation before committing.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required at the decision stage. Presents pricing tiers and plan comparison so startups can evaluate fit against their budget and team size. Positioned after Evidence so the Validator has already internalized proof, making the price feel justified and the choice clear.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Final commitment prompt placed immediately before Recovery per system rules. Delivers a focused CTA to convert to paid, reinforced by the accumulated proof and pricing context. For an organic visitor at this stage, the CTA is direct — 'Start your plan' — with minimal friction.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always-last beat providing secondary paths — contact sales, explore documentation, or revisit comparison — ensuring Validators who aren't quite ready still have a way forward rather than bouncing entirely.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

Required by the decision-stage rules and directly addresses the Validator's need for empirical confirmation before committing.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision stage conversion page requires 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only' to avoid competing with primary conversion moment below.

Validator archetype at decision stage requires 'with-price for decision stage: centered offer presentation with price_display slot surfacing the price as a fact before the CTA' to anchor commitment credibility.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: The semantic intelligence layer for AI-era startups. Components that resolve behavior by archetype, funnel stage, and context — without manual variants.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires 'with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity' and 'Always include role and company for Validator credibility' to establish trust signals.

slots.quote: We shipped a fully intent-aware component system in three days. MODE replaced a month of variant sprawl with a single resolution pass. The palette map alone justified the purchase.

page.reasoning: Validator benefits from 'specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements' and '3-col-icon is the default' for structured capability articulation.

slots.headline: What the intelligence layer actually does
slots.subhead: Six capabilities that replace configuration, manual QA, and Figma handoff cycles — each with a measurable output.

features.title: Semantic Resolution Engine
features.description: Components resolve the correct behavior, copy density, and visual emphasis across four dimensions — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode — without a single manual variant.

features.title: Palette Map
features.description: Visual emphasis accumulates semantically toward conversion. The right weight, contrast, and accent are assigned per component per funnel stage — no manual color assignment ever.

features.title: IA Planner
features.description: Brief to full page architecture in seconds. Powered by Claude Opus, the planner produces a complete section map before your first component renders.

features.title: Multi-Variant Generation
features.description: One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel. Ship the right experience for every segment from a single authoring pass.

features.title: Component Manifest
features.description: 11 components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent. Every component knows where it belongs and why.

features.title: Full Ownership
features.description: MODE lives in your codebase. No runtime dependency, no vendor dashboard, no usage-based billing. Fork it, extend it, make it yours — the intelligence layer is infrastructure you control.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires 'Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust' to establish credibility.

stats.label: Median time from install to first working build
Reduction in design-to-code QA cycles reported by early adopters
Faster page architecture versus manual component planning
stats.source: Internal benchmark, IA planner vs. unassisted, 2024

page.reasoning: Decision stage conversion requires 'highlighted variant for recommended plan' and 'Validator variant includes description and longer feature list' to guide the conversion decision with clarity.

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Resolution engine, component manifest, token config, palette map, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI — in your codebase, not a vendor tool.
features: Three palette presets — funnel, archetype, feature-emphasis
Token dashboard with editable palette map
Lifetime access — no subscription, no lock-in

page.reasoning: Validator archetype at decision stage requires 'Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required')' to reinforce confidence at the conversion moment.

slots.headline: The semantic layer your design system is missing.
slots.subhead: One purchase. Runs in your pipeline. Resolves the right experience for every user, every stage, every time.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. No subscription. No runtime dependency.

page.reasoning: Conversion page requires 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows' to recover abandoning visitors.
